response:
The blocking security-audit CI failure was caused by a pinned vulnerable dependency. We reproduced the failure locally, discovered aiohttp==3.13.3 in both functions/requirements.txt and functions-webhook/requirements.txt triggered pip-audit GHSA findings, and fixed it by bumping aiohttp to the first patched release:

- Updated functions/requirements.txt and functions-webhook/requirements.txt to aiohttp==3.13.4
- Re-ran pip-audit on both requirement manifests (no known vulnerabilities reported)
- Ran the test suite (./.venv-3.13/bin/pytest functions/tests – 454 passed)

As part of hardening the pipeline we also removed the deprecated Safety scan and left pip-audit as the blocking dependency scanner.
context_injected:
--- Episode 1 [codex 2026-04-06] (similarity: 0.6647) ---
In fleet-platform-app on 2026-04-06, reproduced a failing security-audit CI dependency gate locally when GitHub Actions inspection was blocked by Rackspace SAML on the gh token. Root cause was aiohttp pinned to 3.13.3 in both functions/requirements.txt and functions-webhook/requirements.txt; pip-audit reported 10 GHSA advisories with first fixed version 3.13.4. Updated both manifests to aiohttp==3.13.4, reran pip-audit for functions and webhook requirements with no known vulnerabilities found, and ran ./.venv-3.13/bin/pytest functions/tests -q successfully (454 passed).

--- Episode 2 [codex 2026-04-06] (similarity: 0.6232) ---
On 2026-04-06 in fleet-platform-app, removed Safety from the security pipeline after confirming pip-audit remains the correct blocking dependency scanner and bandit/ruff do not cover package CVEs. Updated .github/workflows/security-audit.yml to install only pip-audit, removed both informational Safety scan steps, and stopped uploading safety-results.json. Removed safety==3.7.0 from functions/requirements-dev.txt. Verification: no remaining safety references in the active workflow or dev requirements.

--- Episode 3 [codex 2026-03-27] (similarity: 0.6139) ---
For fleet-platform-app PR #74 on 2026-03-27, the failing GitHub 'Pre-Commit (Gate)' job was not a broad hook miss; CI ran file-scoped pre-commit on changed files and failed only on Bandit B110 in functions/shared/deployment_conflicts.py due to two bare except/pass blocks. Fixed by removing the unnecessary try around response.text extraction and narrowing response.json exception handling to (AttributeError, TypeError, ValueError). Verified with `PRE_COMMIT_HOME=/tmp/pre-commit-cache ./.venv-3.13/bin/pre-commit run --files functions/shared/deployment_conflicts.py --color=always`, then committed as e95db3c and pushed to dev.

--- Episode 4 [codex 2026-03-27] (similarity: 0.5814) ---
For azr-fleet-platform PR #70 on 2026-03-27, failing Test Suite jobs were caused by GitHub Actions service container startup failing before checkout, not product tests. Root cause from run 23655768486: Docker Hub rate limiting on postgres:15-alpine with error 'toomanyrequests: too many failed login attempts for username or IP address'. Fixed locally in commit 1277cce by replacing postgres:15-alpine with public.ecr.aws/docker/library/postgres:15-alpine in .github/workflows/test.yml and the analogous test jobs in deploy-fleet-dev.yml and deploy-fleet-prod.yml. Left unrelated local change in .github/workflows/security-audit.yml uncommitted.

--- Episode 5 [codex 2026-03-19] (similarity: 0.547) ---
On 2026-03-19, PR #55 in fleet-platform-app had a failing Governance WAF GitHub Actions workflow caused by three workflow issues, not product code: (1) the manifest job hard-failed when AZR_COMPLIANCE_V3_REPOSITORY was unset, (2) cross-repo checkout of racker/azr-compliance-v3 failed without a dedicated AZR_COMPLIANCE_V3_READ_TOKEN secret, and (3) the workflow invoked python -m pytest without installing pytest. Fixed on branch dev with commits 48fa34b, 8747bd8, bd90e4f, and 0b173f9. Final workflow behavior: use racker/azr-compliance-v3 as default repo, skip external manifest verification with a warning when no read token is configured, and install pytest before running manifest unit tests.

--- Episode 6 [codex 2026-03-19] (similarity: 0.5332) ---
On 2026-03-19 in fleet-platform-app, committed two related changes on branch dev. Commit 34a738f adds a governance WAF policy pipeline: listener-scoped governance WAF Bicep, route manifest generation/validation from azr-compliance-v3, smoke tests, and GitHub Actions workflow governance-waf.yml. Commit 8dc2f2e hardens the isolated webhook hot path against timeout storms in functions-webhook by adding in-memory token cache with background refresh, bounded DB and Service Bus timeouts, sender reuse, returning 503 on enqueue failure for retries, and raising host timeout to 5 minutes as a band-aid. Working tree was clean after both commits.

--- Episode 7 [codex 2026-03-23] (similarity: 0.5279) ---
On 2026-03-23 for azr-fleet-platform PR #66, CI and review fixes were prepared locally before push. CI failure was caused by OpenAPI 3.1 Event Grid GET response schemas using nullable in api/openapi.yaml; fixed by converting those fields to type unions with 'null' so scripts/strip-v1-prefix.py generated a valid api/openapi-apim.yaml. Four live Copilot review comments were validated and fixed: (1) ama_assign_policies now distinguishes no_entitled_ama_policies from missing_entitled_ama_dcrs and returns missing_dcr_os_types; (2) select_policies_for_subscription no longer duplicates resolve_subscription_policy_assignments and derives policy keys from get_expected_artifacts_for_service_blocks; (3) http_eventgrid_subscription_get now validates customer/subscription ownership via customer_subscriptions before DB/Azure fallback and returns 404 on mismatch; (4) Azure fallback upsert now persists event_delivery_schema. Targeted tests passed: test_http_eventgrid.py, test_ama_targeting.py, test_misc_resource_change_baseline.py, and pre-commit passed. Local commits: 516f83a (Fix Event Grid OpenAPI null schemas), 8ea4b54 (Address PR 66 review findings).

--- Episode 8 [codex 2026-03-27] (similarity: 0.5188) ---
Implemented and validated two production regression fixes in fleet-platform-app on branch dev: (1) AMA DCR deployment now treats transient ARM conflicts like 'Data collection rule has been modified before operation completed' as retryable, waits for the canonical Rackspace DCR to materialize with the expected rax-config-version, and reuses it if present; tests in functions/tests/test_ama_create_dcr_conflicts.py. (2) Bulk callback path no longer sends source_run_id in governance registration payload, preventing downstream FK failures against bulk_automation_runs, and Janus callback handling now treats target-state-equals-current-state as an idempotent skip instead of a mismatch warning; tests updated in functions/tests/test_bulk_callback_client.py and functions/tests/test_policy_rule_events.py.

--- Episode 9 [codex 2026-03-23] (similarity: 0.518) ---
On 2026-03-23 in fleet-platform-app, a live public API smoke test before commit showed that the main API route families /v1/runs, /v1/drift/runs/{id}, and /v1/compliance/checks/{id} were working through APIM, but Event Grid read routes were not fully exposed publicly. Direct backend checks on azr-fleet-platform-prod proved these backend routes work: GET /api/v1/customers/{customerId}/eventgrid/subscriptions and GET /api/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid both returned 200. The public APIM endpoints for those same reads returned 404 because api/openapi-apim.yaml did not expose GET on /customers/{customerId}/eventgrid/subscriptions and did not include /customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid at all. The internal validation-status endpoint returned 401 directly because it uses Function auth level and is not part of the public bearer-auth API contract. Repo fix was to add the missing GET operations to api/openapi.yaml and api/openapi-apim.yaml, while keeping validation-status treated as internal-only.

--- Episode 10 [codex 2026-03-23] (similarity: 0.5157) ---
On 2026-03-23 in azr-fleet-platform PR #67, five Copilot review findings were validated and fixed in commit 12fdec5. Fixes: AMA remediation start endpoint now generates a DB run_id up front and passes it into the orchestration so statusUrl/logsUrl use /v1/runs/{runId} instead of the Durable instance id; remediation timeout minutes are coerced/clamped to 1-240 via _coerce_remediation_timeout_minutes in both onboarding remediation loops; resolve_entitled_ama_os_types now returns a sorted list; http_ama 500 responses now return a stable safe message plus correlationId instead of raw exception text; explicit target_os_types in ama_setup_subscription now raise on invalid values instead of collapsing to [] and later expanding to both OSes. Targeted tests passed and all PR #67 review threads were replied to and resolved.
